name: Trade Flow Exhaustion (Extreme Threshold)
hypothesis: An extremely high buyer-initiated trade flow (z-score > 3.5) represents a high-probability exhaustion point where liquidity providers absorb the aggressive flow, leading to a mean reversion (price drop) over the next 20 ticks. This is a direct test of the parent spec's conviction limit.
mutation_note: Based on feedback to 'tighten_threshold'. Tightened threshold from 3.0 to 3.5 to test if a more extreme Z-score isolates a higher-conviction subset of trades and finds the signal's performance peak.
